title: secale cereale var. anatolicum
intro: Secale cereale var. anatolicum, also known as Anatolian rye, is a subspecies of rye (Secale cereale) native to the Anatolian Peninsula in Turkey. It is a perennial grass that has been traditionally used for its medicinal and health-promoting properties. The plant is known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Anatolian rye can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in Turkey to treat various ailments.
